titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Elizabeth Hart Frazier and Donald Hart Frazier Papers
Elizabeth Hart Frazier and Donald Hart Frazier Papers
titlestmt.author: Abigail Kromminga

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS).

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 5, 2024 at 22:01

did.unittitle: Elizabeth Hart Frazier and Donald Hart Frazier Papers
did.abstract: Extensive writings from Donald Hart Frazier (YDS B.D. 1938) and Elizabeth Hart Frazier (YDS B.D. 1940) reflect over a half century of parish ministry and concern issues of family planning and the rights of women, which they engaged through their involvement in Planned Parenthood, the United Church of Christ, and Yale Divinity School. Substantial gathered materials, including meeting minutes, agendas, memos, and publications from Planned Parenthood (World Population, League of Connecticut, and League of Greater New Haven) from the 1960s to 1980s, provide insight into abortion rights advocacy and the evolving feminist movement in church and society.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Elizabeth Hart Frazier and Donald Hart Frazier Papers (RG 331), Special Collections, Yale Divinity Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Donald Hart Frazier was born in 1911, in Forksville, Pennsylvania. After attending high school in Vermont, he enrolled at Oberlin College, where he received his B.A. in 1933. While at Oberlin, he met Elizabeth Hart, born in 1915 in Massillon, Ohio. Following her graduation from Oberlin in 1936, Donald and Elizabeth married, and she joined him in New Haven at Yale Divinity School. Donald graduated from Yale Divinity in 1938 and Elizabeth in 1940, while six months pregnant with the first of their four children.

As Donald pastored Congregational churches throughout the Northeast—in Connecticut, Vermont, New Jersey, and back in the New Haven area at the First Congregational Church of West Haven in 1960—Elizabeth cared for the family, led religious education programs, provided pastoral care, and served on various community boards, including the Board of Directors of Family Service of New Haven, Planned Parenthood League of New Haven and Connecticut, and as president of the West Haven PTA. After nearly thirty years in parish ministry, Donald transitioned into development work for the United Church of Christ and later Albertus Magnus College.

The call to ordination came to Elizabeth through her nonprofit, advocacy work. In the fall of 1970, Church Women United (of the National Council of Churches) appointed Elizabeth to the Religious Advisory Committee of Planned Parenthood. Upon walking into her first meeting, she was confronted with a group of religious leaders she later described as, "all male, all agonizing over the female body." Prompted by her understanding of the value she brought to this work as an educated woman, and the requirement for ordination to serve with the Clergy Abortion Counseling Service, Elizabeth was ordained into the United Church of Christ in 1972.

In the 1970s and 80s, Donald and Elizabeth shared fourteen interim pastorates in Connecticut, and continually engaged with the social issues of their time. They shared a passion for the responsible stewardship of capital (a topic they jointly published on in the 1940s) and a concern for an ever-changing and expanding world. Attention to world population, the rights of women, and the growth of healthy families saturated their words from the pulpit and their work over a half century in the United Church of Christ, Planned Parenthood, and other organizations.

In 1991, in view of his approaching anniversary of fifty years in ordained ministry, Donald Hart Frazier wrote to the UCC conference minister requesting that Elizabeth be acknowledged with him. "She could have been ordained for the same fifty years as I," he explained, "she deserves to stand with us to be recognized. I recognize this is an unusual request but ours has been an unusual relationship. I have not functioned alone but rather with a fully committed and fully trained partner."
Following Donald's death in 2000, Elizabeth continued the work from their lifetime together: supporting local organizations, speaking publicly, and writing. She died at the age of 104 in 2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Donald and Elizabeth Hart Frazier Papers reflects the ways in which the Hart Fraziers engaged with the pressing questions of their time regarding ministry, population, and women: through writing, preaching, researching, and extensive organizational involvement—processes which amassed (as reflected in this collection) a corpus of writings from over a half century, correspondence, and varied materials from meeting minutes and conference notes to newspaper clippings.

Series I, Writings, dating from around 1938 to 2015, contains two main subseries: the gathered writings of Elizabeth Hart Frazier and of Donald Hart Frazier. Elizabeth's writings begin with essays from her time at Yale Divinity School and thereafter jump into the 1970s, following her ordination in 1972. The sermons topically reflect her advocacy work regarding family planning and population concerns. The subseries of Elizabeth's writings ends with published material, often creative, some autobiographical, in form.

Donald Hart Frazier's writings begin with his notes from Yale Divinity courses and proceed into sermons and addresses from his ministerial life. Notable within the sermons and addresses is his "General Association of Connecticut Moderator's address, 'Women in Our Common Life,'" box 3, folder 11. The address led to the 1971 pronouncement, written with Elizabeth, presented the synod of the UCC. The published writings, some jointly authored with Elizabeth, include writings on financial stewardship and religious educational texts.

Series II, Materials related to Yale, dating from 1925 to 2017, contains materials from their days at the Divinity School and their continued financial and intellectual commitment to the school. The series begins with materials relating to the Divinity School, containing class booklets reflecting on their years at the Divinity School to materials relating to the Hart Fraziers' work as class agents fundraising. The second subseries contains materials relating to the wider University. Notable within the series is "Correspondence related to women at YDS and curriculum development," box 5, folder 6, containing materials related to Elizabeth's involvement in assessing the YDS curriculum resulting in the 1971 lecture series, "Search for Livable Lifestyles."

Series III, Family planning and reproductive rights, contains materials dating from 1962 to 2018. The series contains subseries relating to work with Planned Parenthood and other organizations, and topical files containing a variety of materials: clippings, pamphlets, and circulars that Elizabeth Hart Frazier gathered. Folders titled "Programmatic materials" include meeting minutes, member lists, conference schedules, and correspondence. The subseries Planned Parenthood is organized by branch of the organization: the wider national organization, World Population and the Religious Advisory Committee, Planned Parenthood League of Connecticut, and Planned Parenthood League of Greater New Haven. These files provide detailed insight into the strategy and functioning of Planned Parenthood from circa 1965-1975 and particularly considering a global boom in population.

Notable within the other organizations are the subseries "Clergy Counseling" and "The Religious Coalition for Abortion Rights." Formed before the national legalization of abortion through Roe v. Wade in 1973, Clergy Counseling trained religious leaders to counsel and support women in family planning. The files in box 8, folder 11-14, contain training and informational materials and Elizabeth Hart Frazier's counseling notes. In the aftermath Roe v. Wade in 1973 and the ensuing opposition from the Catholic Church, Jewish and Protestant members of Clergy Counseling formed the Religious Coalition for Abortion Rights.

Series IV, Church related materials, dating from 1959 to 1996, begins with a subseries related to the United Church of Christ, including substantial materials on women in the UCC, providing insight into the experiences of clergywomen and denominational attitudes towards the feminist and reproductive rights movements. Series IV proceeds to programmatic materials, publications, and correspondence from the CT Fellowship of Congregational Women (affiliated with the UCC) and Church Women United (affiliated with the National Council of Churches), women's groups that Elizabeth was active in. The final subseries contains miscellaneous church-related materials, from a study by the World Council of Churches on women in ministry in 1963 (box 11, folder 11), to gathered materials from interim pastorates.
Series V, Other organizations, dating from 1964 to 2014, contains materials from organizations the Hart Fraziers were involved in and is arranged alphabetically by organization.

Series VI, Subject files, dates from around 1940 to the late 2010s. The materials vary in format, from magazines and circulars to newspaper clippings, and reflect broadly the collecting interests and research of Elizabeth Hart Frazier: women in ministry and the church, the nascent feminist movement and its evolution, the wellbeing of families and modern society. Similar material is in Series III, Family planning and reproductive rights, "Topical files," including Elizabeth's extensive research on global population.

Series VII, Biographical documentation, dating from 1945 to 2016, holds resumes, autobiographical writings and reflections, personal correspondence, and newspaper articles about Donald and Elizabeth Hart Frazier.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "My philosophy of life" and ""The philosophy of Felix Adler."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also, Biographical documentation, Series VII, "Materials relating to Elizabeth Hart Frazier's ordination," box 14, folder 16.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials regarding women, religion, and family planning.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing "The Wakeup Call," 1994, "The Appalachian Trail: Grandma's Perspect," 2000, and "Paper on Planned Parenthood," n.d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Hidden Goals, Unseen Hopes," and "A self study IRS style."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: AAUW poetry publication Tide Lines (2009), and The Diamond Project: Ordinary Women Leading Extraordinary Lives (2010), including Elizabeth Hart Frazier's essay "Two Defining Hours."

did.unittitle: Course notes from YDS classes Christian Ethics and Old Testament
Course notes from YDS class New Testament
General Association of Connecticut Moderator's address, "Women and Our Common Life"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also, Church Related Material, Series IV, "Pronouncement "Women in Our Common Life,"" box 10, folder 13.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including works jointly authored with Elizabeth Hart Frazier in the serial Congregational Connecticut and Congregational Vermont .

did.unittitle: Year Book of the Union Church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the pastor's report written by Donald Hart Frazier.

title.part: Paul Speaks to the Modern Church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Pilgrim Press (Boston and Chicago).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily from the Missionary Herald and Pilgrim Youth . Including the articles, "Socialism, Communism, and Capitalism," "The Historical Significance of the Life and Work of Jesus," and "The Theology of the Gospel of John."

did.unittitle: Uniform series: International Bible Lessons for Christian Teaching
Class of 1940 "The Age of the Giants"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Compiled by Graham R. Hodges, class of 1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Booklet, handout, and written letter regarding the Yale Divinity School women's organization.

did.unittitle: Correspondence related to women at YDS and curriculum development

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including correspondence from Elizabeth Hart Frazier to Dean Colin Williams, "Report of the Committee on the Ministry of Women Convened Under the Directive of the Alumni Council of YDS," 1970, and correspondence regarding the public forum "Search for Livable Lifestyles," Fall 1971.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous materials related to women at YDS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Educational Policy Minutes," June 10, 1932 memo related to the acceptance of women at YDS, posters and newspaper clippings from the 1971 and 1972 lecture series and Elizabeth Hart Frazier's written account of organzing the programming, and notes regarding women's studies at YDS.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing drafts, notes, and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing the proposal, correspondence, and ensuing publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Religious Proposal for World Order," by Liston Pope, 1941, sermons by Colin Williams, and "Roland Bainton and Elizabeth Feist Hirsch," n.d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding giving to YDS and Yale, including drafts of class agent letters written by Elizabeth Hart Frazier and Donald Hart Frazier.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Flowers for Ruth: Fiftieth Reunion Comments," 1990, and "Reflection: A Poem," 1994 by Elizabeth Hart Frazier.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials relating to the acceptance of women into Yale College in 1969.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly materials regarding Planned Parenthood annual meetings. Including the booklet "Planned Parenthood: Objectives, Activities and Structure for the 1970s," and "An Introduction to Planned Parenthood Federations of America, inc."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles reprinted and distributed by Planned Parenthood from Cosmopolitan , Essence , and Good Housekeeping .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Planned Parenthood Report , Research Notes , and International Planned Parenthood News .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing "Legal Abortion in the United States: Facts and Highlights," 1973?, "Annual Report," 1975, "Planned Births, the Future of the Family and the Quality of American Life," 1977.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including bumper stickers and "Graphic Resource List" of images available for order.

did.unittitle: Planned Parenthood Indian River and Vero Beach

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "George W. Bush's War on Women," 2003.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "The Affiliate Handbook," planning materials, and committee resolutions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Today's Churches and Family Planning," n.d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Planned in affiliation with Planned Parenthood World Population.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including conference proposal, "Sexism: the Price and the Penalty in Family Planning and Religion."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials published and distributed by Planned Parenthood World Population. "Message on Population and Family Planning," by President Richard Nixon, 1969. World Population newsletters. Materials relating to family planning and mental illness inlcuding, "The Role of Family Planning in the Primary Prevention of Mental Illness," by Richard A. Shwartz, 1969, and on religious attitudes towards family planning and abortion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including getting it together… the newletter of the College Program, Planned Parenthood-World Population.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A service in cooperation between the National Council of Churches and Planned Parenthood-World Population.

did.unittitle: Related to sexual education series in New Haven

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including outlines and notes from the series "Clinical Aspects of Sexual Behavior," held through the Yale School of Medicine.

did.unittitle: Planned Parenthood League of Greater New Haven

title.part: For Caseworkers: Experience in Counseling Birth Control

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handbook published by Planned Parenthood of Colorado.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including counselor standards and guidelines.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Information on abortion procedures throughout the duration of a pregnancy and referral advice.

did.unittitle: The Religious Coalition for Abortion Rights

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An organization run out of Yale Law School.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including information on abortion law in Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Reproductive Freedom Task Force - New Haven Feminist Union

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing materials from the conferences "Unwed Parents: A Community Challenge," 1966, "Connecticut Speaks on Sex Education: Pro and Con," 1966, "Problems and Solutions: the Liberation of Women," 1971.

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings on abortion and the court
Articles and clippings on birth control

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handouts, articles, statements, and a poster on Christianity and issues of sexual education, family planning, and abortion.

did.unittitle: Materials from the Department of Family Planning Government of India
Booklet on family planning in India

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Due to size, this booklet is housed in box 15.

did.unittitle: United Church Board and UCC miscellaneous correspondence
Council for Lay Life and Work and Church Leader's Institute

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including correspondence, speeches, and the writings of Donald Hart Frazier from his time leading the commission.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: News service of the United Church of Christ.

did.unittitle: United Church of Christ and women

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pronouncement written by Donald and Elizabeth Hart Frazier to be presented to the 1971 synod. Including drafts and extensive correspondence.

did.unittitle: Statements and information on abortion, women, sexuality

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Member lists, reports, programs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including correspondence, proposals, and Elizabeth Hart Frazier's application to mentor UCC clergywomen attending Andover Newton Theological School.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing correspondence and circulars related to the United Church of Christ Coordinating Center for Women In Church and Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing editions on abortion and women in church and society and further correspondence on the 1971 abortion edition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publication of the UCC Coordinating Center for Women in Church and Society.

did.unittitle: UCC women in ministry newsletters and reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also called United Church Women, a service of the National Council of Churches (NCC).

did.unittitle: New Haven Council of Church Women

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including reports "Insights from the Sex Morality Committee," "Resolution on Abortion" (1970), and "The Status of Women in the Church" (1969).

did.unittitle: WCC reports on women in ministry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From the World Council of Churches (WCC) Department on the Cooperation of Men and Women in Church, Family and Society.

did.unittitle: Related to interim pastorate in Waterbury and history

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including materials related to curriculum and conferences on women. Minimal materials relating to Donald Hart Frazier's time directing the development office.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Elizabeth Hart Frazier was president of the New Haven branch, 1978-1979.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also Writings, Series I. Box 2, folders 10-12.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing FOCUS newsletter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including training in advocacy, member lists, annual reports, and financial statements.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing committee minutes, board agendas, correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including workshop descriptions and proposals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also Writings, Series I. Poetry, box 2, folder 10.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials relating to media reform work and the task force on women and religion.

did.unittitle: Status of women, visit to the UN, notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials relating to International Women's Year regarding the labor and status of women in Connecticut and the United States. Including materials from the United States Department of Labor.

did.unittitle: International Year of the Child conference

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Conference held at Yale University, including conference materials and Elizabeth Hart Frazier's notes.

did.unittitle: International Year of the Child publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Elizabeth Hart Frazier served as a board member 1972-1984.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: YWCA working group.

did.unittitle: Magazines and proposals: women in ministry and church
Newspaper clippings: women in ministry and church
Gathered materials: United Church of Christ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials on feminism and women's liberation distributed by KNOW inc. Including, "Autobiographical Notes Relevant to Sex Descrimination in Academic Psychology," Jo-Ann Evans Gardner, 1968, "Training the Women to Know Her Place: The Power of a Nonconscious ideology," 1970?, "Women in the Seventies: Problems and Possibilities," Alice S. Rossi, 1970, "Sex-Segregated Want Ads: Do They Discourage Female Job Applicants?," n.d.

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings: domestic violence, sexual assault

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Group Homes for Indian American Children in North and South Dakota," 1964.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Perspectives On Busing," Inequality in Education , no.11 (1972).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written by the vice president of New Haven Rail Road, suggested by Elizabeth Hart Frazier.

did.unittitle: Donald Hart Frazier biographical statements, writings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gathered resumes written by Donald Hart Frazier and including "Center Church Remarks Fifty Years In the Ministry," and his self-written obituary.

did.unittitle: Elizabeth Hart Frazier biographical statements, writings
Materials relating to Elizabeth Hart Frazier's ordination

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including the writings of Elizabeth's ordination paper and Donald's "charge to the church," notes, and correspondence regarding the ordination.

did.unittitle: Brides magazine with Elizabeth Hart Frazier interview

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including material from Temple Shalom of West Essex, co-founded by Donald Hart Frazier (and correspondence related thereto) and materials related to important installations, services, and events.
